cb_step_1.title: Select Pelvic CT Volume
cb_step_1.simple: Choose the Pelvic Volume from the Input CT Volume dropdown.
cb_step_1.detailed: The pelvic CT scan is the foundation for all subsequent segmentation and planning. This step ensures the correct imaging dataset is loaded for fracture analysis. In the 'Input CT Volume' option, select the Pelvic Volume from the dropdown menu.

cb_step_2.title: Segment the Pelvis
cb_step_2.simple: Click the 'Run Step 1: Segment Pelvis' button to segment the pelvic bone from the CT.
cb_step_2.detailed: This step automatically segments the intact pelvic bone from the surrounding anatomy. The resulting pelvic segmentation will serve as the anatomical reference for fracture reduction and screw planning. Press the 'Run Step 1: Segment Pelvis' button and wait for the computation to finish.

cb_step_3.title: Segment the Fracture Fragments
cb_step_3.simple: Click the 'Run Step 2: Segment Fractures' button to identify and separate the fracture fragments.
cb_step_3.detailed: This step automatically detects and segments individual bone fragments from the fractured pelvis. Accurate fragment segmentation is critical for planning reduction and screw placement. Press the 'Run Step 2: Segment Fractures' button and wait for the computation.

cb_step_4.title: Exclude Unused Fragments
cb_step_4.simple: In the fragments list, untick any fragments you wish to exclude from the surgical plan.
cb_step_4.detailed: Some small or non-displaced fragments may not require reduction or screw fixation. Excluding them simplifies the plan and focuses attention on the clinically relevant fragments. Review the listed fragments and uncheck the ones to be excluded; then click Done.

cb_step_5.title: Generate a Reduction Template
cb_step_5.simple: Click the 'Run Step 3: Generate Template' button to create a template based on the intact pelvis.
cb_step_5.detailed: This step uses the original pelvic segmentation to create a template that guides the reduction of fragments back into anatomical alignment. The template provides a target shape for subsequent registration. Press the 'Run Step 3: Generate Template' button.

cb_step_6.title: Register and Reduce Fragments
cb_step_6.simple: Click the 'Run Step 4: Register _Reduce' button to automatically align the fracture fragments to the template.
cb_step_6.detailed: This step computes the optimal alignment of each fracture fragment to the template pelvis, effectively reducing the fracture virtually. The result is a reduced pelvic model that simulates the post-operative anatomy. Press the 'Run Step 4: Register _Reduce' button.

cb_step_7.title: Manual Adjustment Needed?
cb_step_7.simple: If the automatic reduction looks good, skip to step 12. If adjustments are needed, tick the 'Manually adjust a fragment' checkbox.
cb_step_7.detailed: The automatic reduction may not be perfect for all fragments. If you are satisfied with the alignment, proceed to screw planning. Otherwise, enable manual adjustment to fine-tune individual fragment positions. Tick the checkbox only if you need to make adjustments.

cb_step_8.title: Select Fragment to Adjust
cb_step_8.simple: In the 'Fragment' selection box, choose the fragment you want to adjust.
cb_step_8.detailed: You can adjust one fragment at a time. Pick the fragment from the dropdown list. This selection will activate the transform tools for that fragment.

cb_step_9.title: Adjust Fragment Position and Rotation
cb_step_9.simple: Use the transform widget to move and rotate the selected fragment into the desired position.
cb_step_9.detailed: Using the 3D Slicer transform tools, translate and rotate the fragment to improve its alignment with the template pelvis. This manual step allows fine-tuning of the reduction. When you are satisfied with the position, click Done.

cb_step_10.title: More Adjustments?
cb_step_10.simple: If you need to adjust more fragments, choose Yes. Otherwise, choose No to proceed.
cb_step_10.detailed: After adjusting one fragment, you may need to adjust others. Select Yes to return to the fragment picker and continue, or select No to finalize all manual adjustments.

cb_step_11.title: Apply Manual Adjustments
cb_step_11.simple: Click the 'Apply adjustments' button to save the manual fragment repositioning.
cb_step_11.detailed: This step locks in the manual adjustments you made, updating the reduced pelvis model with your fine-tuned fragment positions. Press the 'Apply adjustments' button.

cb_step_12.title: Plan Screw Placements
cb_step_12.simple: Click the 'Run Step 5: Plan Screws' button to automatically suggest screw trajectories.
cb_step_12.detailed: This step analyzes the reduced pelvis and fracture lines to propose optimal screw positions and trajectories for fixation. The suggested screws will be displayed as cylindrical models. Press the 'Run Step 5: Plan Screws' button.

cb_step_13.title: Edit Screw Trajectories?
cb_step_13.simple: If you are satisfied with the planned screws, you are done. If you need to adjust screw trajectories, tick the 'Edit Screw trajectories' checkbox.
cb_step_13.detailed: The automatic screw planning may need refinement for patient-specific anatomy. If adjustments are required, enable edit mode to manually modify screw positions and orientations. Tick the checkbox only if you need to edit screws.

cb_step_14.title: Adjust Screw Trajectories
cb_step_14.simple: Use the transform tools to adjust the position and orientation of the screw trajectories.
cb_step_14.detailed: Manually fine-tune each screw's entry point, direction, and length using the transform widget. Ensure screws are placed within safe bone corridors and avoid critical structures. When all screws are satisfactory, click Done.

cb_step_15.title: Regenerate Screws
cb_step_15.simple: Click the 'Regenerate screws from edited lines' button to update the screw models based on your manual edits.
cb_step_15.detailed: After manually adjusting the screw trajectories, this step recreates the screw models to match the edited lines. This ensures the visual representation reflects your modifications. Press the 'Regenerate screws from edited lines' button.

cb_step_16.title: Finalize Screw Plan
cb_step_16.simple: Untick the 'Edit Screw trajectories' checkbox to exit edit mode and confirm the plan.
cb_step_16.detailed: This step locks in your screw edits and exits the adjustment mode. The final screw plan is now ready for review or export. Untick the checkbox to complete the workflow.
